title: silene littorea var. nana
intro: Silene littorea var. nana, also known as dwarf silene or dwarf catchfly, is a small flowering plant species of the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of Silene littorea var. nana typically involve infusions or teas, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
